depression.description: I often feel overwhelmed by the expectations my family places on me, especially regarding my wedding. It makes me anxious and worried about disappointing them.
I constantly struggle with feelings of inadequacy, especially when I compare myself to my father's behavior. I worry that I am not living up to what my family expects.
Setting boundaries with my family is really hard for me. I feel guilty when I try to distance myself, even though I know it's necessary for my well-being.

Delusion.description: I have this constant fear that if I don't meet my parents' expectations, they will abandon me. It drives me to seek their approval even when it hurts.
I see myself mirroring my father's behavior in seeking approval, and it scares me. I worry that I'm stuck in this cycle and can't break free.

psychosis.description: I feel a lot of anxiety when I think about family dynamics, especially when it comes to my wedding. I fear that any disagreement will lead to conflict and rejection.

psychosis.topic: Conflicted feelings about loyalty to family
psychosis.description: I often feel torn between my loyalty to my family and my own needs. I worry that if I prioritize my own happiness, I will be seen as selfish or unloving.
I find it difficult to be emotionally vulnerable with my family. I fear that if I show my true feelings, they will judge me or not understand.
